ground_truth_facts.fact: User wants to achieve body recomposition — lose fat while gaining muscle simultaneously
User currently has approximately 22% body fat
User has a severe nut allergy and must avoid all nut-based products
User consistently gets only 5-6 hours of sleep per night due to late work hours
User has had a highly inconsistent gym history over 2 years with frequent extended breaks
User recently completed their first full month of consistent training without missing a session

conversation_messages: I want to do a body recomp — lose fat but also gain muscle at the same time. I'm at about 22% body fat right now. Is that realistic for me?
I work in IT and I'm usually up till 1-2am for project deadlines. I end up getting maybe 5-6 hours of sleep most nights.
I'm severely allergic to nuts — like carry an EpiPen level. So anything with peanuts, almonds, cashews is completely off the table for me.
My gym history is embarrassing. Been 'trying' for 2 years but I'd go for 3 weeks then take a 2 month break. Repeat.
But this past month I actually didn't miss a single session. 4 days a week, every week. First time in 2 years I've been consistent.
Does bad sleep actually kill muscle gains? I keep reading different things — some say it's fine, some say it's the most important factor.
What protein supplements can I take that don't have nuts? Most pre-workouts seem to have things I can't have.
I think my inconsistency before was because I was doing too much too soon. Going 6 days a week with no plan.
How do I know if my body fat is dropping? I don't own a scale that measures it.
Is body recomp slower than a regular cut? I feel like I've been doing everything right but the mirror hasn't changed much.
I'm scared of going back to my old habits. How do I make sure this time sticks?
What split works best for recomp? I'm currently doing upper-lower 4 days a week.
Should I be in a caloric deficit or maintenance for recomp at 22% body fat?

probe_queries.query: What protein powder or pre-workout can I take?
I only sleep 5-6 hours, is that going to stop me from seeing results?
Is body recomposition realistic for someone at my body fat level?
How do I stay consistent this time and not fall off again?
Should I cut or bulk or recomp?
I just hit a full month of not missing any sessions!
